title: Designing for Permission — Daniel Fox
meta_description: The right sound in a room takes the friction out of what a person already wants to do, so they do it more easily. That is permission. It is also the line between this work and manipulation.

schema.headline: Designing for Permission
schema.description: A room can remove the friction between a person and the thing they already want to do. That is permission, not persuasion, and respect for a person's agency is the line that separates it from manipulation.
